subject: everyday-science
description: Galaxies MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is a galaxy?
options: A large collection of stars, dust and gas held together by gravity
mcqs.explanation: A galaxy is a large collection of stars, dust and gas in space — a system of millions or billions of stars held together by gravity.

mcqs.q: What does the light coming from stars indicate about them?
mcqs.explanation: The light coming from stars gives the idea of their rotational speed and temperature.

mcqs.q: Why do constellations appear at different positions during the night?
mcqs.explanation: The constellation of stars appears at different positions in the sky at different times during night mainly due to Earth's rotation.

mcqs.q: What do sunspots cause?
options: Aurora australis, aurora borealis (polar auroras) and geomagnetic storms
mcqs.explanation: Sunspots cause aurora australis and aurora borealis (polar auroras) and geomagnetic storms on the surface of the Sun.

mcqs.q: What is the closest star to Earth other than the Sun?
mcqs.explanation: Proxima Centauri is the closest star to Earth other than the Sun — about 4.25 light years away.

mcqs.q: How far is Proxima Centauri from Earth?
mcqs.explanation: Proxima Centauri is about 4.25 light years from Earth.

mcqs.q: Praesepe (Messier 44 / M44 / Beehive Cluster) is located in which constellation?
mcqs.explanation: Praesepe (also called the Beehive Cluster or M44) is a large, bright open star cluster located in the constellation Cancer.

mcqs.q: The change in colour of stars arises due to:
mcqs.explanation: The change in colour of stars arises due to variation in their surface temperature — hotter stars appear blue, cooler stars appear red.

mcqs.q: Polaris (the North Star) lies at the end of the handle of which constellation?
mcqs.explanation: Polaris, the North Star, lies at the end of the handle of the Little Dipper — its stars are faint compared to those of the Big Dipper.

mcqs.q: What type of galaxy is the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The Milky Way is an example of a barred spiral galaxy.

mcqs.q: What is the diameter of the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The diameter of the Milky Way is 100,000 to 180,000 light years.

mcqs.q: What is the distance from the Sun to the Galactic Centre of the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The distance of the Sun to the Galactic Centre of the Milky Way is 27,000 light years.

mcqs.q: How old is the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The Milky Way is approximately 13.6 billion years old — nearly as old as the universe itself.

mcqs.q: How many stars are in the Milky Way (range)?
mcqs.explanation: There are 100 billion stars in the Milky Way on the low-end estimate and 400 billion on the high-end estimate.

mcqs.q: In which constellation is the Milky Way's central bulge located?
mcqs.explanation: The constellation of the Milky Way's central bulge is Sagittarius.

mcqs.q: The Milky Way is which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: The Milky Way is our home galaxy — a barred spiral galaxy containing the Sun and our entire solar system.

mcqs.q: How is the Milky Way held together?
mcqs.explanation: Like all galaxies, the Milky Way is held together by gravity.

mcqs.q: Approximately how many light years does the Milky Way extend through?
mcqs.explanation: The Milky Way extends through a distance of 10⁵ (100,000) light years.

mcqs.q: What is the closest LARGE galaxy to the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy (M31) is the closest large galaxy to the Milky Way.

mcqs.q: What is another name for the Andromeda Galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is also known as M31 (Messier 31).

mcqs.q: What type of galaxy is the Andromeda Galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is an example of a spiral galaxy.

mcqs.q: How far is the Andromeda Galaxy from Earth?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is located about 2,480,000 light years (2.48 million light years) from Earth.

mcqs.q: What is the diameter of the Andromeda Galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The diameter of the Andromeda Galaxy is approximately 200,000 light years — about twice the Milky Way.

mcqs.q: How many stars does the Andromeda Galaxy contain?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy contains approximately 1 trillion stars.

mcqs.q: Which is described as the BRIGHTEST galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is the brightest galaxy (visible to the naked eye in dark skies).

mcqs.q: How many globular clusters orbit in and around the Andromeda Galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: There are at least 450 globular clusters orbiting in and around the Andromeda Galaxy.

mcqs.q: How many dwarf galaxies accompany the Andromeda Galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is accompanied by 14 dwarf galaxies — including M32, M110, and possibly M33 (the Triangulum Galaxy).

mcqs.q: What type of galaxy is Messier 87?
mcqs.explanation: The type of Messier 87 is elliptical.

mcqs.q: Messier 87 is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Messier 87 is also known as Virgo A or NGC 4486, generally abbreviated to M87.

mcqs.q: What is the diameter of Messier 87?
mcqs.explanation: The diameter of Messier 87 is 120,000 light years.

mcqs.q: How far is Messier 87 from Earth?
mcqs.explanation: The distance of Messier 87 from Earth is 54 million light years.

mcqs.q: In which constellation is Messier 87 located?
mcqs.explanation: The constellation of Messier 87 is Virgo.

mcqs.q: M87 is famous as the home of:
options: Several trillion stars, a supermassive black hole and ~15,000 globular star clusters
mcqs.explanation: M87 is the home of several trillion stars, a supermassive black hole, and a family of roughly 15,000 globular star clusters.

mcqs.q: The Antennae galaxies are classified as:
mcqs.explanation: The type of Antennae is Interacting Spirals — two spiral galaxies currently colliding.

mcqs.q: What is the tail-to-tail distance of the Antennae galaxies?
mcqs.explanation: The tail-tail distance of the Antennae is 350,000 light years.

mcqs.q: In which constellation are the Antennae galaxies located?
mcqs.explanation: The constellation of the Antennae is Corvus.

mcqs.q: The Magellanic Clouds are classified as a:
mcqs.explanation: The Magellanic Clouds are a Disrupted Barred Spiral. The Small Magellanic Cloud is more specifically irregular.

mcqs.q: What is the diameter of the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC)?
mcqs.explanation: The diameter of the Large Magellanic Cloud is 14,000 light years.

mcqs.q: What is the diameter of the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC)?
mcqs.explanation: The diameter of the Small Magellanic Cloud is 7,000 light years.

mcqs.q: In which constellations is the Large Magellanic Cloud located?
mcqs.explanation: The constellation of the Large Magellanic Cloud is Dorado and Mensa.

mcqs.q: In which constellations is the Small Magellanic Cloud located?
mcqs.explanation: The Small Magellanic Cloud is an irregular type galaxy in constellations Tucana and Hydrus.

mcqs.q: What is the mass of the Large Magellanic Cloud?
mcqs.explanation: The mass of the Large Magellanic Cloud is 10 billion solar masses (M☉).

mcqs.q: What is the mass of the Small Magellanic Cloud?
mcqs.explanation: The mass of the Small Magellanic Cloud is 6.5 billion solar masses (M☉).

mcqs.q: Which galaxy is described as a spiral galaxy?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy (M31) is an example of a spiral galaxy.

mcqs.q: Which galaxy is described as elliptical?
mcqs.explanation: Messier 87 is an elliptical galaxy.

mcqs.q: Which galaxy is the brightest as observed from Earth?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is the brightest galaxy.

mcqs.q: The Andromeda Galaxy has a:
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy has a very crowded double nucleus.

mcqs.q: The Milky Way's age is closest to:
options: The age of Earth (4.5 billion years)
The age of the Universe (~13.8 billion years)
Half the age of the Universe
mcqs.explanation: The Milky Way is about 13.6 billion years old — nearly as old as the universe (~13.8 billion years).

mcqs.q: Which is the closest large galaxy to the Milky Way?
mcqs.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy (M31) is the closest large galaxy to the Milky Way.

mcqs.q: M87 is the home of approximately how many globular star clusters?
mcqs.explanation: M87 is the home of a family of roughly 15,000 globular star clusters — far more than the Milky Way's ~150.

mcqs.q: The Milky Way contains how many stars (per the source's high-end estimate)?
mcqs.explanation: There are 100 billion stars in the Milky Way on the low-end and 400 billion on the high-end estimate.
